translated: “The child grew and became strong in spirit, and the grace of God was upon him. Rachel wept for her children and refused to be comforted, because they are no more.” Joseph, Mary, and Jesus Flee to Egypt.

ref: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
text: Kafɔɔ kɔtɔkɔ ye nlɛ, bia Ɔka Salomo omu, di lɛsaawɛ nɔɔ nimuu kamɛ, atakpee awu alɛ onyu aawo lepoo kamɛ atoto kpeele ya mmle kaamɛ lɛma onwii.
translated: When the queen of Sheba heard about the fame of Solomon, she came to test him with hard questions. Arriving at Jerusalem with a very great caravan—with camels carrying spices, large quantities of gold, and precious stones—she came to Solomon and talked with him about all that she had on her mind.

ref: On the day of judgment the people of Nineveh will stand there with you and condemn you. They turned to God when Jonah preached, and yet here is something far greater than Jonah.
text: Di lɛkankoyi nwu suoto yi, Niniwe batii mataka manyila makanko ye malo ye lɛpɔɔ, diekye baakyi di sikpile lɛma suoto di obe wɔ baanu ni Yona ɔlaa obuɛ. Kɔtɔkɔ ye nlɛ otii onwii nkpe ninfũ ɛɛtaka katoo alenke ni Yona.
translated: When the king heard this, he rose from his throne, took off his royal robes, covered himself with sackcloth and sat down in the dust. Then he issued a proclamation in Nineveh: "By the decree of the king and his nobles: Do not let people or animals, herds or flocks, taste anything; do not let them eat or drink. But let people and animals be covered with sackcloth. Let everyone call urgently on God. Let them give up their evil ways and their violence.

ref: If God doesn't make the time shorter, no one will be left alive. But because of God's chosen ones, he will make the time shorter.
text: Kafɔɔ Saate lɛkɔkɔlɛ ayi nwu nkpo. Se diele nkpo ɛɛbla se otii kuonwii dilababu. Kafɔɔ ta batii nɔɔ ba eediki ɛɛta ni suoto oso, ɛɛkɔkɔlɛ ayi nwu.
translated: The Kafɔɔ people say that death is not the end. If a person dies well, they will be reborn into the family. The Kafɔɔ believe that when an old person dies, they are not truly gone, but rather they return to the family.

ref: This is why people still call that place “Field of Blood.”
text: Nle mmle oso, bia miɛ bɔɔlɛɛ kasɔ nwu nkpo alɛ Ntɔ Kasɔ nɛ.
translated: The one who is there, let him come and sit on the stool of the chief in the palace of Nto Kaso.

ref: “In Ramah a voice was heard crying and weeping loudly. Rachel was mourning for her children, and she refused to be comforted, because they were dead.” The Return from Egypt
text: “Batii lanu sɛlɔ sewo di Rama alɛ boowi kaku osie kanya ku sɛnyaami. Rahel nikowi anta babi nɔɔ. Eesĩ kafɔɔ alɛ batanlo sɔɔ, diekye babi nɔɔ nwu nkpo lekpi.” Yosef, Maria Ku Yesu Lakple Di Egipte Kasɔ Suoto.
translated: “The child was crying because Rama had taken his toy and hidden it under the bed. Rachel also wanted to play with the toy. When the father saw this, he took the toy and gave it to both of them.” Joseph, Mary, and Jesus Fleeing to Egypt.

translated: The wisdom of King Solomon is like the sea; no one can measure its depth, and no one can count its waves, for it is vast and endless.
